text: ‘A welcome-offering’; literally, in the Latin, ‘an offering of things that make peace’. The Hebrew text is understood by some as implying a sacrifice made for the ‘peace’, that is, welfare, of the worshipper; by others as implying thanksgiving. But the implication seems to be that offriendship; the sacrifices thus described were distinguished from others by the fact that the worshipper himself shared the sacrificial meal, as if by way of hospitality, instead of devoting all to the service of the sanctuary.
